title: Recovery from abandoned signup
scenario_context: User starts signup, abandons mid-flow. Email reminder with resume link.
reasoning: Save partial state on email entry. Send 1d, 3d, 7d emails with magic-link to resume.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Partial save. 2) Bounded reminders. 3) Opt-in.

title: 2FA enforcement per tenant
scenario_context: Enterprise tenant requires 2FA for all members. New users prompted to enroll.
reasoning: Tenant setting. Force enroll on next login if missing. Backup codes. Recovery via admin.
frontend_solution: // 2FA enroll wizard with QR + backup codes
test_strategy: Tenant requires; user enrolls + logs in
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Backup codes. 2) Admin recovery. 3) Grace period.

title: Recharts dynamic y-axis domain
scenario_context: Auto-fit y-axis to data with padding. Default zero-anchored can squish.
reasoning: Compute min/max, add 10% padding, round to nice. Memoize.
frontend_solution: // const domain = useMemo(() => { const min = Math.min(...vals); const max = Math.max(...vals); const pad = (max-min)*0.1; return [Math.floor((min-pad)/10)*10, Math.ceil((max+pad)/10)*10]; }, [vals]);
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Padded. 2) Nice numbers. 3) Memoized.

title: Visx tooltip with portal
scenario_context: Tooltip near edge clipped by overflow:hidden parent.
reasoning: @visx/tooltip useTooltipInPortal handles portal + position.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Portal pattern. 2) Scroll-aware. 3) Position math handled.

title: Chart.js with screen-reader fallback table
scenario_context: Canvas chart inaccessible. Provide HTML table fallback.
reasoning: Render same data as <table>. visually-hidden by default; available via SR. Toggle button shows visibly.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Table ships. 2) Visually-hidden. 3) Toggle reveals.

title: ECharts with offline export to image
scenario_context: User wants PNG of chart for report. Server-side render not available.
reasoning: chart.getDataURL({ pixelRatio: 2, backgroundColor: '#fff' }) → blob → download.
frontend_solution:
// const url = chart.getDataURL({ pixelRatio: 2, backgroundColor: '#fff' });
// const a = document.createElement('a'); a.href = url; a.download = 'chart.png'; a.click();
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Native API. 2) HiDPI. 3) Background color.

title: Tremor with role-based card visibility
scenario_context: Admin sees revenue card; member doesn't. Use Can.
reasoning: Server-protected endpoint; UI shows Card only with right perm.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Server enforced. 2) UI gated. 3) Layout stable.

title: Comparing time periods (vs previous period)
scenario_context: Show this month vs last month. Need overlay or side-by-side?
reasoning: Overlay 2 lines, period-aligned x-axis. Tooltip shows both + delta.
frontend_solution: // LineChart with two Lines, second dashed
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Aligned x-axis. 2) Delta tooltip. 3) Dashed for previous.

title: Adding markdown rendering: sanitize + plugins + storage
scenario_context: Comments support markdown. Need: parse, sanitize, render, store raw.
reasoning: Store raw. On render, parse to AST, sanitize. react-markdown with rehype-sanitize.
backend_solution: // server uses unified pipeline for indexing
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Store raw, render safe. 2) AST sanitize. 3) Whitelist.

title: Cascading failure: timeout config per dependency
scenario_context: DB slow → app slow → load balancer marks unhealthy → restart → repeat. Need bounded timeouts.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single slow query hangs request thread.
reasoning: Per-dependency timeouts: DB 3s, Stripe 5s, internal 1s. Total request timeout enforced.
backend_solution: // every external call wrapped with AbortController + timeout
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Bounded per dep. 2) Total budget. 3) Fail fast.

title: Generated TypeScript from GraphQL schema
scenario_context: GraphQL API. Hand-written types drift. Use codegen.
reasoning: GraphQL Code Generator. Types regenerated on schema change. Plugins for client (Apollo, urql, TanStack).
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Codegen. 2) Schema source. 3) Per-client typed hooks.

title: Per-tenant SLA tracking and reporting
scenario_context: Enterprise contracts include SLA (99.9% uptime). Track per-tenant.
reasoning: Log incidents per tenant. Compute monthly. Auto-credit per contract.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Per-tenant. 2) Auto-credit. 3) Public status page.

title: Multi-currency invoicing
scenario_context: Customers in different currencies. Invoices in their currency. Exchange rates locked at invoice creation.
reasoning: Currency at customer level. Invoice in customer currency. Display Intl.NumberFormat with currency.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Locked rate. 2) Customer-currency invoice. 3) HQ reporting alt.

title: Onboarding state via reducer
scenario_context: Multi-step state best modeled as reducer not multiple useStates.
reasoning: useReducer for complex multi-field state. Actions descriptive.
frontend_solution: // const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, init); dispatch({ type: 'setName', name });
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Reducer for complex. 2) Actions named. 3) Pure → testable.

title: Login attempts: lockout + alert
scenario_context: Failed logins on account. Lock after 5 + alert user.
reasoning: Failed attempts counter per account. Lock 15min after 5. Alert user. Unlock via password reset.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Lockout + alert. 2) Email-gated unlock. 3) IP + account scope.

title: Recharts referenceLine for thresholds
scenario_context: Show SLA threshold on latency chart. Above is bad.
reasoning: <ReferenceLine y={threshold} stroke='red' label='SLA' />
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Threshold visible. 2) Labeled. 3) Color encodes severity.

title: Visx with stream graph
scenario_context: Show categorical changes over time. Stream more aesthetic than stacked bar.
reasoning: d3.stack with offset wiggle. Visx area per series. Tooltip for exact.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) d3.stack offsets. 2) Right use case. 3) Tooltip exact.

title: Chart.js custom interaction modes
scenario_context: Default hover finds nearest x. Sometimes want nearest XY.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Scatter with index mode shows wrong tooltip.
reasoning: interaction.mode 'nearest' + axis 'xy' for scatter; 'index' for time series.
frontend_solution: // options: { interaction: { mode: 'nearest', axis: 'xy', intersect: false } }
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Mode matches data. 2) Sensible defaults. 3) Touch-friendly.

title: ECharts polar chart for time-of-day patterns
scenario_context: Hourly activity over a day. Polar reveals cycles.
reasoning: polar with angle 0-24h. Bar or line series.
frontend_solution: // option = { polar: {}, angleAxis: { type: 'category', data: hours }, radiusAxis: {}, series: [{ type: 'bar', coordinateSystem: 'polar', data: ... }] }
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Polar for cyclic data. 2) Time-of-day clear. 3) Compact.

title: Tremor with print-friendly settings
scenario_context: Dashboard often printed. Print CSS optimizes.
reasoning: Hide nav, force light, expand grid, break-inside-avoid.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Print first-class. 2) Force light. 3) break-inside-avoid.

title: Chart with explicit empty/zero distinction
scenario_context: No data vs zero data are different. Show explicitly.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User wonders if metric is broken.
reasoning: State 1: no records (empty). State 2: records exist but sum to zero. Each different message.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Semantic distinction. 2) Specific UX. 3) Helpful CTAs.

title: Adding global search across resources
scenario_context: Search bar searches projects, members, files, etc. Each scoped to tenant.
verification_chain: Layer 0: search index per resource type
reasoning: Parallel search per resource type. Combined sorted results grouped by type.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Parallel. 2) Grouped. 3) Keyboard.

title: What if message queue is full — backpressure
scenario_context: Producer faster than consumer. Queue fills. Need backpressure.
reasoning: Queue bounded. Producer checks depth. Slow at threshold. Alert ops.
backend_solution: // BullMQ/pg-boss has built-in; check depth before enqueue
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Bounded. 2) Backpressure to producer. 3) Alert.

title: Schema versioning with migration helpers
scenario_context: Old localStorage data uses v1 schema. Need migrate to v2 transparently.
reasoning: Wrap data: { version: 2, data }. Migration table v1→v2.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Versioned. 2) Chained migrations. 3) Logged.

title: Tenant migration tooling: split / merge
scenario_context: Sometimes need to split a tenant (separate divisions) or merge two acquired companies. Need tooling.
reasoning: Plan + dry-run + actual + verify. Lock both during. Audit. Atomic per record where possible.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Dry-run. 2) Lock during. 3) Audit + verify.

title: Plan grandfathering
scenario_context: Customers on old plan kept on old price. New customers pay new.
verification_chain: Layer 3: new plans don't affect existing
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No way to grandfather new sign-ups
reasoning: Stripe price IDs immutable. New customers use new price ID. Existing keep old.
frontend_solution: // plan picker shows current price (theirs vs new)
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Immutable prices. 2) Per-sub price. 3) MRR by version.

title: Onboarding personalized via signup answers
scenario_context: Ask role + use-case at signup. Personalize subsequent steps.
reasoning: Ask 2-3 questions. Branch flows accordingly.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Used not collected. 2) Few questions. 3) Per-profile funnel.

title: Compliance: encrypted at rest + key rotation
scenario_context: Sensitive fields encrypted in DB. Rotate keys without downtime.
reasoning: AWS KMS / GCP KMS envelope encryption. Data key per record. Master key rotates. Re-encrypt data keys lazily.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Envelope encryption. 2) KMS-managed. 3) Per-record data key.

title: Recharts XAxis with custom tick component
scenario_context: Tick labels need icons or rich formatting.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Can't add icon next to label.
reasoning: <XAxis tick={<CustomTick />} />. Custom component receives payload + position.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Composable. 2) SVG-native. 3) ARIA on text.

title: Visx parallel coordinates for multi-dim data
scenario_context: Compare items across 5+ numeric dimensions. Parallel coords > radar for many.
reasoning: d3-scale per axis. Lines connect points. Brush per axis for filtering.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Right chart for high-dim. 2) Brushing. 3) Axis reorder.
